07 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Trump threatens Venezuela with ground military operations IRNA 07 Dec 2025 -- Tensions between Venezuela and the United States have escalated further as President Donald Trump says that ground operations against alleged drug trafficking networks in the South American nation will start 'very soon.'

Defense Policy / Programs

  • U.S. to deter China through strength, not confrontation: Pete Hegseth CNA 07 Dec 2025 -- United States Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Saturday that one of the U.S. military's main priorities is to deter China "through strength, not through confrontation," as he reiterated Washington's stance that it has no intention of changing the Taiwan Strait status quo.

  • Iran says new US National Security Strategy is effectively written for Israel Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman says a new US National Security Strategy (NSS) outlining Washington's principles and priorities for foreign policy is essentially a security document for the Israeli regime.

  • US Won't Put 'Boots On The Ground' In Ukraine - Trump's Envoy Kellogg Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- Russia and the United States are underway aligning the "difficult provisions" that will determine the future document on Ukraine settlement, Kremlin aide Yury Ushakov said earlier.

  • Japan-Australia Defense Ministers' Joint Statement - Establishment of the Framework for Strategic Defence Coordination Ministry of Defense of Japan 07 Dec 2025
  • Exclusive satellite images show Japan accelerating militarization of its southwestern island Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- Exclusive satellite images recently obtained by the Global Times show that Japan is rapidly advancing military construction on Mageshima Island, a key strategic location in the southwestern region of Japan. Multiple military facilities have taken shape on this uninhabited island in just one year, with the outline of a full-scale base already emerging.
  • Japan's claims of Chinese carrier-based aircraft directing radar onto Japanese warplanes falsely smear normal operations; China expresses strong dissatisfaction: defense spokesperson Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- China's Liaoningaircraft carrier formation conducted far seas training in the waters east of the Miyako Strait, which is in line with international law and international practices.
  • Radar illumination to Self-Defense Force aircraft by Chinese military aircraft Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 07 Dec 2025
  • Japan Pleads for US Backing as It Ramps Up Militaristic Urge Against China Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- On December 7 in the afternoon, Mr. Funakoshi Takehiro, Vice Minister for Foreign Affairs of Japan, summoned Mr. Wu Jianghao, Ambassador of China to Japan.

  • Chinese ambassador lodged solemn representation with Japan over harassment by Japanese fighter jets against Chinese carrier formation Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- Chinese Ambassador to Japan Wu Jianghao on Sunday lodged a solemn representation and strong protest with Japanese Vice Minister for Foreign Affairs Funakoshi Takehiro over the harassment by Japanese Self-Defense Force (JSDF) aircraft against training by Chinese Navy's aircraft carrier formation, according to a release by the Chinese Embassy to Japan WeChat account.
  • Japan's accusation nothing but a trick of "a-thief-crying-stop-thief": Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 07 Dec 2025 -- "Japan falsely accused China's normal operations, which is nothing but a typical trick of 'a-thief-crying-stop-thief'. We are extremely dissatisfied with and firmly oppose the provocative actions and misleading remarks of the Japanese side," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND), on Sunday.
  • Japan should strictly discipline its front-line operations: Navy Spokesperson PRC MND 07 Dec 2025 -- "Japan's related hypes are completely inconsistent with the facts. We sternly urge the Japanese side to immediately cease its slanderous and defamatory acts against China and strictly discipline its front-line operations," said Senior Captain Wang Xuemeng, spokesperson for the Chinese PLA Navy, on Sunday.
  • Chinese Army calls on Japan to stop its 'slanderous acts' amid tensions Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- A spokesman for the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) has sharply criticized Japan, urging Tokyo to cease its "slanderous actions."
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Japan's Accusation of Chinese Naval Carrier-Based Aircraft Conducting "Radar Illumination" on Japanese SDF Fighter Jets PRC MofA 07 Dec 2025
  • Liaoning carrier formation approached, harassed by Japanese SDF aircraft during fighter training near Miyako Strait; Japan's hypes inconsistent with facts: spokesperson Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- The PLA Navy's Liaoning aircraft carrier formation conducted regular carrier-based fighter jet flight training in the waters east of the Miyako Strait and had made public the training sea and airspace in advance.

  • Chinese, Russian militaries hold 3rd joint anti-missile exercise, working together to jointly reinforce post-World War II order: expert Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- According to a statement of China's Ministry of National Defense on Saturday night, the Chinese and Russian militaries conducted their third joint anti-missile exercise within the territory of Russia in early December. The joint exercise doesn't target any third party and has nothing to do with the current international and regional situation, said the statement.
  • Chinese PLA Navy's 989 Task Group arrives in Indonesia for four-day visit PRC MND 07 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese PLA Navy's 989 Task Group, which is currently on a far-sea comprehensive training mission, arrived at the Port of Tanjung Priok in Jakarta, Indonesia on December 7, 2025, kicking off a four-day friendly visit to Indonesia.

  • HMAS Brisbane returns to Sydney from Regional Presence Deployment Australia DOD 07 Dec 2025 -- Royal Australian Navy Hobart class guided-missile destroyer HMAS Brisbanehas returned to Fleet Base East in Sydney following a successful four-month deployment throughout the Indo-Pacific region.

  • Raksha Mantri dedicates to the nation 125 border infrastructure projects - the most in one go - from Ladakh India PIB 07 Dec 2025 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh, on December 07, 2025, dedicated to the nation 125 strategically significant infrastructure projects of Border Roads Organisation (BRO) - the most to have ever been inaugurated simultaneously - from Ladakh

  • Arabian Sea, successfully conducted an anti-narcotics operation, leading to the seizure of 1, 500 Kilograms of Hashish valued at approximately 3 million US Dollars. ISPR Pakistan 07 Dec 2025

  • IRGC chief calls for upgrading missiles with stealth and AI technologies IRNA 07 Dec 2025 -- IRGC Chief Commander Major General Mohammad Pakpour has emphasized the need to enhance Iran's missiles with stealth technology to strengthen their ability to penetrate enemy defenses.
  • Iran to develop advanced stealth missiles to counter Israeli systems: IRGC chief Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The commander-in-chief of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says Iran must equip its missiles with stealth technology to enhance their ability to penetrate Israel's anti-missile system.
  • Missiles with strike radius beyond Persian Gulf length used in IRGC naval drill: Parliament speaker Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has commanded the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy for successfully conducting a military exercise in the Persian Gulf, stating that new homegrown long-range missiles were employed during the drill.
  • Armed Forces in full intelligence and operational readiness: Top general IRNA 07 Dec 2025 -- Chairman of Iran's Armed Forces Chief of Staff, Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi, emphasized that the Armed Forces are fully prepared in both intelligence and operational capacities.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Netanyahu Announces Soon Transition to 2nd Phase of Trump's Gaza Peace Plan Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said that the first phase of US President Donald Trump's peace plan had already been nearly implemented and that the parties would shortly move on to its second phase, calling for the disarmament of Palestinian movement Hamas and the demilitarization of the enclave.
  • Netanyahu Claims Peace With Palestinians, Arab World Achievable Without Palestinian State Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu once again rejected the idea of establishing the State of Palestine alongside Israel, saying that peace between Israel and the Arab world, including the Palestinians, could be achieved without a Palestinian state.

  • The Chief of the General Staff to the Reserve Troops in the Gaza Strip Israel Defense Forces 07 Dec 2025
  • Dismantling of an Underground Tunnel Route and Weapons-Storage Facility Israel Defense Forces 07 Dec 2025

  • 'History didn't start on Oct. 7': Hamas defends Al-Aqsa Flood, says open to comprehensive ceasefire talks Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- Hamas is ready to adopt a comprehensive approach in the second round of Gaza ceasefire talks, a senior official said Sunday, defending the movement's Al-Aqsa Flood operation as an act of defense in response to decades-long Israeli atrocities.
  • Hamas says will surrender arms to 'Palestinian state' only if Israeli occupation ends Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has said that it will surrender its weapons to a "Palestinian state" only if Israel's occupation fully ends, stressing that disarmament cannot occur under ongoing military and political oppression.

  • Egypt reaffirms 'clear and firm' stance: Rafah will not be a channel for Palestinian displacement Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- Egypt has reaffirmed that it will not allow the Rafah border crossing to be used to displace Palestinians, stressing the need for international monitoring of the current ceasefire amid ongoing Israeli violations.

  • Netanyahu, Blair held secret meeting to discuss future of Gaza: Report Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The Israeli regime's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, has reportedly held a secret meeting with former British Prime Minister Tony Blair to discuss issues regarding the control of Gaza following two years of genocidal war against the besieged territory.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Yemen opens trial for 13 people charged with spying for CIA, Israel Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- A court in Yemen has commenced the trial of 13 individuals on charges of operating a spy network for the US Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) and Israel's Mossad spy agency.

  • Response by the Spokesperson on Remarks by the Indian Minister for External Affairs Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 Dec 2025 -- On media queries regarding the statement made by the Indian External Affairs Minister, the Spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Mr. Tahir Andrabi stated the following:

  • On 06 December 2025, security forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Kalat District of Balochistan, on reported presence of terrorists belonging to Indian proxy, Fitna al Hindustan. ISPR Pakistan 07 Dec 2025

  • One year after Assad's fall, Syria's future must be 'free, sovereign and united' UN News 07 Dec 2025 -- The UN Secretary-General has marked the first anniversary of the collapse of Bashar al-Assad's regime, calling the moment "a day to honor [Syrians'] sacrifices and to renew the aspirations that fueled the historic change in the country."

  • Iran reiterates full sovereignty over trio islands in Persian Gulf IRNA 07 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei says there is "no doubt or skepticism" about Iran's sovereignty over the three Persian Gulf islands of Abu Musa, Greater Tunb, and Lesser Tunb.
  • Qalibaf warns neighbors not to test Iran's resolve over Persian Gulf trio islands Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has dismissed the hackneyed claims about three Iranian islands in the final statement of the Persian Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), warning the neighboring states not to test the Islamic Republic's resolve to safeguard its sovereignty.

  • 100 abducted Nigerian students freed, over 160 remain missing Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- Nigerian authorities have secured the release of 100 schoolchildren abducted by gunmen from a Catholic school last month, a United Nations source and local media confirmed Sunday.

News Reports

  • Benin's armed forces foil coup attempt against President Talon: Authorities Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- Benin's armed forces successfully neutralized an attempted coup d'état against President Patrice Talon over the weekend, authorities said.
  • African Union Condemns Attempted Coup in Benin, Expresses Support for Head of State Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- The African Union on Sunday strongly condemned the attempted coup in Benin and expressed support for the country's current president, Patrice Talon.
  • OIC General Secretariat Condemns Military Coup Attempt in the Republic of Benin Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 07 Dec 2025 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) strongly condemned today's attempted military coup in the Republic of Benin

  • Kremlin views US national security strategy not mentioning Russia as a 'direct threat' as a positive step: media Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- The change of the US national security strategy by the administration of US President Donald Trump, where Russia is not mentioned as a "direct threat," is a positive step, Kremlin Spokesman Dmitry Peskov told TASS on Sunday.
  • Kremlin welcomes US removal of Russia as 'direct threat' from National Security Strategy Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The Kremlin has described the US administration's decision to omit Russia from its list of direct security threats as a constructive shift from the confrontational stance of previous American policies.
  • Amendments in US's New Security Doctrine Largely Align With Russia's Vision - Kremlin Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- The adjustments made to the new US National Security Strategy are largely consistent with Moscow's vision, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Sunday.

  • Macron warns Beijing of tariffs in bid for EU-China trade deal Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- French President Emmanuel Macron has threatened to impose tariffs on Chinese goods in pursuit of a more balanced trade relationship with the global economic powerhouse.
  • Voting for 8th-term LegCo of HKSAR begins; CE says first meeting of new LegCo to focus on relief work of Wang Fuk Court fire Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- The 8th-term Legislative Council Election of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) was held on Sunday, marking another important LegCo election following the improvement of its electoral system and the implementation of the principle of "patriots governing Hong Kong."
  • China's Long March-8A rocket launches new internet satellite group Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- China launched a Long March-8A carrier rocket on Saturday in the southern island province of Hainan, sending a group of internet satellites into space.
  • China's MSS warns of foreign spy agencies collecting public employees' data via setting up malicious Wi-Fi connection Global Times 07 Dec 2025 -- China's Ministry of State Security (MSS) warned in an article published on its WeChat account on Sunday that foreign spy agencies may set up malicious Wi-Fi networks in specific areas near government offices and if public employees accidentally connect to them, the data on their devices could be stolen.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 07 Dec 2025

  • Chairman of SPA Standing Committee Inspects Various Units KCNA 07 Dec 2025 -- Choe Ryong Hae, member of the Presidium of the Political Bureau of the Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea and chairman of the Standing Committee of the Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, inspected various sectors.

  • US envoy admits Washington twice tried and failed to topple Iran's government Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- US Special Envoy for Syria Tom Barrack says Washington attempted twice in the past to overthrow the Iranian government but achieved nothing.
  • Second Group Of Iranian Nationals Deported From US Due To Arrive In Tehran RFE/RL 07 Dec 2025 -- Dozens of Iranian nationals deported from the United States are due to arrive in their home country over the next few hours, the Foreign Ministry in Tehran said on December 7, the second such repatriation under President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.
  • Iran's mega water transfer and coastal industrial pivot Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkianon on Saturday inaugurated one of Iran's most ambitious infrastructure projects for the transfer of water from the Oman Sea to the central plateau, stretching over 800 kilometers from Sirjan to Isfahan.

  • Prime Minister Netanyahu's Remarks at the Joint Press Conference with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz Israel - Prime Minister 07 Dec 2025
  • Netanyahu says won't quit politics even if granted pardon in corruption trial Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The Israeli regime's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, has said that he would not retire from politics if he received a presidential pardon in his years-long corruption trial.
  • White House pushing for Sisi-Netanyahu summit: Report Press TV 07 Dec 2025 -- The White House is making preparations to broker a rare summit between Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi, who have not had direct communication since before the Gaza war.

  • PM call with Prime Minister Schoof of Netherlands: 7 December 2025 Gov.UK 07 Dec 2025

  • Indispensable discussions: Foreign Minister Wadephul travels to China Germany Federal Foreign Office 07 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Wadephul is visiting China for talks on 8 and 9 December and will address important issues while he is there.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for China Germany Federal Foreign Office 07 Dec 2025

  • Press conference by Federal Chancellor Merz and Prime Minister Netanyahu on Merz's inaugural visit to Israel "The friendship between Germany and Israel is precious" Germany - Federal Government 07 Dec 2025

  • Hungary's Paks Nuclear Power Plant Gets Enough Russian Fuel to Run Until 2028 - Szijjarto Sputnik 07 Dec 2025 -- Another shipment of Russian fuel has arrived at Hungary's Paks Nuclear Power Plant, the last one this year, ensuring enough stock until November 2028, Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto said.
